text: The spell of extreme heat this month has seen the UK record for June broken.
zh: 本月的极端高温已经打破了英国六月的记录。

text: The new top temperature for this month was just over 36 degrees, recorded in Gosport in Hampshire.
zh: 本月新的最高气温刚过36度，出现在汉普郡的戈斯波特。

text: The Met Office has extended the current red extreme heat warning, meaning a risk to life, until the end of tomorrow.
zh: 英国气象局已将当前的极端高温红色预警延长至明天午夜，意味着存在生命危险。

text: It now covers more parts of southern England across Dorset, Hampshire and Sussex.
zh: 该预警现已覆盖英格兰南部更多地区，包括多塞特、汉普郡和苏塞克斯。

text: It's also issued another amber warning for heat for Friday, covering eastern and central England.
zh: 气象局还针对周五发布了另一份高温琥珀色预警，覆盖英格兰东部和中部地区。

text: People stranded in heavy traffic for hours following a serious crash between a van and lorry on the M25 have been treated for heat -related illnesses.
zh: 在M25公路上一辆货车与卡车发生严重相撞事故后，被困在拥堵车流中数小时的人们已因中暑相关疾病接受治疗。

text: Rail passengers were told not to travel unless it was completely essential.
zh: 铁路乘客被告知，除非绝对必要，否则不要出行。

text: More than 1 ,000 schools in England and Wales were shut or closed early because of today's soaring temperatures and there's more on the way.
zh: 英格兰和威尔士有超过1000所学校因今天的高温而停课或提前放学，而且高温还将持续。

text: Helena Wilkinson reports.
zh: 海伦娜·威尔金森报道。

text: Sunrise this morning and many parts of the UK were braced for a day of soaring temperatures.
zh: 今天清晨日出时，英国许多地区都在为高温天做准备。

text: The impact today across many parts of our lives has been severe.
zh: 今天的高温对我们生活的许多方面都造成了严重影响。

text: This morning, commuters struggled with the already uncomfortable heat under the blazing sun.
zh: 今天早上，通勤者在炙热的阳光下艰难应对本已难耐的高温。

text: Rail passengers faced cancellations and delays and were warned not to travel to, from or within the red weather zone unless absolutely necessary.
zh: 铁路乘客面临取消和延误，并被警告除非绝对必要，否则不要前往、离开或经过红色预警区域。

text: Tonight, South East Coast Ambulance Service are treating patients stranded on the M25 for heat -related illnesses after an earlier serious crash.
zh: 今晚，东南海岸救护车服务正在为早些时候因严重车祸而被困在M25公路上、出现中暑相关症状的患者提供治疗。

text: Many have been stuck in long queues for hours.
zh: 许多人已在长长的车队中被困数小时。

text: And Queen Alexandra Hospital in Portsmouth has declared a critical incident after a cooling system supporting critical infrastructure failed during the heatwave.
zh: 朴茨茅斯的亚历山德拉女王医院在热浪期间因支持关键基础设施的制冷系统故障，已宣布进入重大事件状态。

text: Many schools have been without pupils today.
zh: 今天许多学校都没有学生上课。

text: In England and Wales more than a thousand were shut or closed early because of the extreme heat.
zh: 在英格兰和威尔士，有超过一千所学校因极端高温而停课或提前放学。

text: This school in Cardiff was closed at lunchtime and will be shut tomorrow.
zh: 卡迪夫的这所学校在午餐时间停课，明天也将停课一天。

text: The classrooms on one side of the building are quite really bad with temperature at the moment so we decided that it was the safest thing to do because
zh: 大楼一侧的教室目前温度非常高，所以我们认为这样做最安全，因为

text: it's not comfortable for the pupils and because of that they don't really learn that much anyway
zh: 这样的环境对学生来说很不舒服，而且他们本来也学不进去多少东西

text: so we decided for everybody's health and well -being to just close this afternoon and close all day tomorrow six -year -old harris's school in west london will also be closed tomorrow
zh: 所以我们决定，为了大家的健康和福祉，今天下午停课，明天全天也停课。六岁的哈里斯在伦敦西部就读的学校明天也将停课。

text: his mother understands why but is frustrated it is really tough i was relatively lucky this week i didn't have too many fixed meetings but you know I need to be in the office most days a week.
zh: 他的母亲理解这一决定，但也感到很无奈：这真的很艰难，我这周相对幸运，没有太多固定的会议，但你知道，我每周大部分时间都得去办公室上班。

text: The biggest risk here is that you know this isn't a one -off I don't think it's
zh: 这里最大的风险在于，这并不是一次性的事件，我认为

text: going to keep occurring and so we do need some sort of a plan and a strategy around it and probably some funding to support the schools because they just can't cope right now.
zh: 这种情况还会持续发生，所以我们确实需要某种计划和策略，也可能需要一些资金来支持学校，因为它们现在真的应付不来。

text: Some people have been able to work from home today but for others working outside has been unavoidable.
zh: 今天有些人得以在家办公，但对另一些人来说，在户外工作是不可避免的。

text: Horrible it's not very nice there's no shades, you've got no let up.
zh: 太糟糕了，一点都不好受，没有阴凉，根本没法缓一缓。

text: Yeah, you can see it out here, it's baking.
zh: 是的，你在这里就能看到，简直像在烘烤一样。

text: As the high temperatures continue, vulnerable people in particular are being urged to take care.
zh: 随着高温持续，尤其呼吁弱势群体多加小心。

text: It's just about making sure that you are staying as cool as you can.
zh: 关键是要尽量让自己保持凉爽。

text: You know, if you've got, if it's cooler outside, keep your windows open, keep your curtains closed during the day if it's sun facing.
zh: 如果外面比较凉快，就打开窗户；如果是朝阳的一面，白天就把窗帘拉上。

text: If you've got a fan at home, stick it on, stick an ice bowl in front of it.
zh: 如果家里有风扇，就打开它，再在前面放一盆冰块。

text: Lots of cool baths.
zh: 多泡凉水澡。

text: If you've got young children, get the paddling pool out in the shade and let them play.
zh: 如果家里有小孩，就把戏水池搬到阴凉处让他们玩耍。

text: just doing fun things, but things that you can to keep yourself cool.
zh: 做一些有趣的事情，但要选那些能帮你保持凉爽的活动。

text: Even the King was feeling the heat today as he attended a London Climate Week reception at St James's Palace.
zh: 就连国王今天在圣詹姆士宫出席伦敦气候周招待会时也感到了炎热。

text: Luckily, a royal aide was close by with a fan to keep him cool.
zh: 幸运的是，一位王室随从就在旁边，用扇子为他扇风降温。

text: The red warning for extreme heat in parts of England and Wales is due to continue until just before midnight on Thursday.
zh: 英格兰和威尔士部分地区的极端高温红色预警将持续到周四午夜前。

text: with little respite ahead people have been urged to stay safe in the extreme conditions
zh: 由于短期内几乎没有缓解迹象，人们被呼吁在极端天气下注意安全。

text: Helena Wilkinson BBC News while Europe has also struggled to cope with a record -breaking heatwave so far France Spain and Italy have been hardest hit
zh: 海伦娜·威尔金森，BBC新闻报道。与此同时，欧洲也在努力应对一场破纪录的热浪，目前法国、西班牙和意大利受灾最为严重。

text: today was the hottest June day on record for Spain in one region Cantabria the mercury hit nearly 44 Celsius the highest temperature on record for any month of the year.
zh: 今天是西班牙有记录以来六月最热的一天，在坎塔布里亚地区，气温接近44摄氏度，创下全年任何月份的最高纪录。

text: France has had its hottest day since records began in 1947.
zh: 法国迎来了自1947年有记录以来最热的一天。

text: The temperature reached over 40 Celsius in Paris.
zh: 巴黎气温超过了40摄氏度。

text: Several landmarks were closed or shut early and thousands of schools were closed across the country for at least part of the day.
zh: 多个地标景点关闭或提前关闭，全国数千所学校当天至少部分时间停课。

text: Red alerts are in place for 16 cities in Italy including Rome, Milan, Florence and Turin.
zh: 意大利有16个城市发布了红色预警，包括罗马、米兰、佛罗伦萨和都灵。

text: At St. Peter's Square in the Vatican hundreds Hundreds of people waited in the intense heat to see the Pope using whatever they could, shawls, fans and umbrellas to protect themselves from the heat.
zh: 在梵蒂冈的圣彼得广场，数百人在酷热中排队等待觐见教皇，他们用披肩、扇子和雨伞来遮挡烈日。

text: So why are we experiencing such unusually hot weather?
zh: 那么，为什么我们会经历如此异常的高温天气呢？

text: Well, our climate editor, Justin Rowlatt, is in Gosport, which recorded the hottest temperature in the UK today.
zh: 我们的气候编辑贾斯汀·罗兰特正在戈斯波特，那里今天记录到了英国最高气温。

text: Justin.
zh: 贾斯汀。

text: It certainly did.
zh: 确实如此。

text: It was a brutal 36 .1 degrees Celsius, as you say.
zh: 正如你所说，气温高达骇人的36.1摄氏度。

text: That's the provisional figure, a full half degree hotter than the previous June record.
zh: 这是初步数据，比此前的六月纪录还要高出整整半度。

text: And people often say, do these tumbling temperature records really matter?
zh: 人们常常会问，这些不断被打破的高温纪录真的有那么重要吗？

text: We've always had hot days.
zh: 我们一直都有炎热的日子。

text: We've always had heat waves.
zh: 我们也一直都经历过热浪。

text: And they are right up to a point.
zh: 在某种程度上，他们说得没错。

text: Yes, we've always had heat waves.
zh: 是的，我们一直都有热浪。

text: We know why they happen.
zh: 我们也知道它们为什么会发生。

text: High pressure, clear skies, light winds, lots of sunshine.
zh: 高压系统、晴朗天空、微风、充足的阳光。

text: Heat really builds.
zh: 热量因此不断累积。

text: But take a look at this.
zh: 但请看看这个。

text: chart shows the rise in global temperatures since the pre -industrial era.
zh: 这张图表显示了自工业化前时代以来全球气温的上升情况。

text: You've probably seen it before.
zh: 你可能以前见过这张图。

text: and look at how the temperatures take off in the 1970s.
zh: 看看气温是如何在1970年代开始飙升的。

text: And so we now get the same weather patterns, but in that warmer world.
zh: 所以我们现在遇到的天气模式其实和以前一样，只是发生在这个更温暖的世界里。

text: And it means the heat waves are hotter, they last for longer and they're more frequent.
zh: 这意味着热浪更热、持续时间更长、发生频率也更高。

text: I mean, think about it.
zh: 想想看。

text: In a world without climate change, you wouldn't expect temperature records to be broken, but every single one of the last four decades has been hotter than the last.
zh: 在没有气候变化的世界里，你不会预期气温纪录被打破，但过去四十年中，每一个十年都比前一个更热。

text: So I've said it before, Rita, and I'm sure I'll say it again.
zh: 所以我以前说过，丽塔，我相信我还会再说一次。

text: Yes, this is weather, but this is weather in a warming world.
zh: 是的，这是天气，但这是发生在一个正在变暖的世界里的天气。
